title: primula denticulata subsp. erythrocarpa
intro: Primula denticulata subsp. erythrocarpa, also known as the crimson-flowered or red-flowered alpine primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. This plant is primarily used in traditional medicine, where its roots and leaves are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Primula denticulata subsp. erythrocarpa include its potential to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, the plant's roots and leaves are prepared as herbal teas or infusions, or dried and used in capsules or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
